queries: وَإِذْ قَالَ لُقْمَانُ لِابْنِهِ وَهُوَ يَعِظُهُ يَا بُنَيَّ لَا تُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ
يَا بُنَيَّ أَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ وَأْمُرْ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ وَٱنْهَ عَنِ ٱلْمُنْكَرِ وَٱصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا أَصَابَكَ
يَا بُنَيَّ إِنَّهَا إِنْ تَكُ مِثْقَالَ حَبَّةٍ مِنْ خَرْدَلٍ
إن الله كتب الإحسان على كل شيء فإذا قتلتم فأحسنوا القتلة وإذا ذبحتم فأحسنوا الذبح وليحد أحدكم شفرته وليرح ذبيحته
وليشحذها عن ذبيحته فإذا ذبح أحدكم فليستأتر وليأكل وليعطي جاره
يا بني إنها إن تك مثقال حبة من خردل لقمان 16
إن الله كتب الإحسان على كل شيء وليحد أحدكم شفرته وليرح ذبيحته مسلم

# Let's read the file content
path = "/mnt/data/file-3Se37Jdv5k14FQgdj8jcEv-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:2000]

# Find positions of double quotes and extract spans
indices = [i for i, ch in enumerate(text) if ch == '"']
indices, len(indices)

# Extract substrings between quotes and their start/end indexes (excluding quotes)
spans = []
for i in range(0, len(indices), 2):
    start_quote = indices[i]
    end_quote = indices[i+1]
    start = start_quote + 1
    end = end_quote  # end is exclusive
    span_text = text[start:end]
    spans.append((start, end, span_text))
spans

queries: كتب الإحسان على كل شيء فإذا قتلتم فأحسنوا القتلة

output.code:
import re

text = open("/mnt/data/file-3Se37Jdv5k14FQgdj8jcEv-text_to_analyse.txt","r",encoding="utf-8").read()
# Extract spans again
indices = [i for i, ch in enumerate(text) if ch == '"']
spans = []
for i in range(0, len(indices), 2):
    start_quote = indices[i]
    end_quote = indices[i+1]
    start = start_quote + 1
    end = end_quote
    span_text = text[start:end]
    spans.append((start, end, span_text))
# For first six "يا بني ", find [لقمان: N] soon after the end
aya_nums = []
for i,(start,end,span_text) in enumerate(spans[:6]):
    # find match starting at end up to some characters ahead, maybe 30
    # We'll search the bracket pattern after the quote
    m = re.search(r"\[لقمان:\s*(\d+)\]", text[end:end+30])
    if m:
        aya_nums.append(int(m.group(1)))
    else:
        aya_nums.append(None)
aya_nums

import json

# Build the citations list
citations = []
# Aya entries
aya_nums = [13,14,17,18,19,20]
for i,(start,end,span_text) in enumerate(spans[:6]):
    citations.append({
        "span_start": start,
        "span_end": end,
        "span_text": span_text,
        "citation_type": "Aya",
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {"sura_name":"لقمان","sura_number":31,"aya_number":aya_nums[i]},
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    })
# Hadith entries - 3
for start,end,span_text in spans[6:]:
    citations.append({
        "span_start": start,
        "span_end": end,
        "span_text": span_text,
        "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {},
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    })

print(json.dumps({"citations": citations}, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
